symptoms: Presence of ketones in the urine.
Feeling irritable or having other mood changes.
Getting a lot of infections, such as gum, skin and vaginal infections.
causes: A group of diseases that affect how the body uses blood sugar (glucose).
The main cause varies by type, but no matter what type you have, it can lead to excess sugar in the blood.
Prediabetes happens when blood sugar levels are higher than normal.
prevention: Taking steps to prevent prediabetes and gestational diabetes
data.summary: Diabetes mellitus refers to a group of diseases that affect how the body uses blood sugar (glucose). It can lead to excess sugar in the blood, which can cause serious health problems.

symptoms: Chest pain, chest tightness, chest pressure and chest discomfort (angina)
Pain in the neck, jaw, throat, upper belly or back
Pain, numbness, weakness or coldness in the legs or arms if the blood vessels in those body areas are narrowed
causes: Buildup of fats, cholesterol and other substances in and on the artery walls (plaque)
Atherosclerosis (ath-ur-o-skluh-ROE-sis) - a buildup of plaque in the arteries
data.summary: Heart disease describes a range of conditions that affect the heart, including blood vessel disease, irregular heartbeats, and heart valve disease. Symptoms depend on the type of heart disease and can include chest pain, shortness of breath, and pain in the neck or arms.

symptoms: Trouble sleeping caused by shortness of breath, coughing or wheezing
Coughing or wheezing attacks that are worsened by a respiratory virus
causes: Inflammation and swelling of the airways in the lungs
Excess mucus secretion in the airway linings
prevention: Avoiding triggers such as dust, mold, and pet dander
Using a peak flow meter to monitor lung function
Taking medication as prescribed by your doctor
Maintaining good hygiene and avoiding secondhand smoke
data.summary: Asthma is a condition in which the airways narrow and swell, producing extra mucus that makes breathing difficult. Symptoms can vary from person to person and may include shortness of breath, chest tightness, wheezing, and coughing. Asthma cannot be cured, but its symptoms can be controlled with medication and lifestyle changes.

A stroke occurs when the blood supply to the brain is interrupted, either due to a blockage (ischemic stroke) or bleeding (hemorrhagic stroke). Prompt treatment can reduce brain damage and improve outcomes.
Diabetes is a group of diseases that affect how the body uses blood sugar. Symptoms include increased thirst and urination, weight loss, blurred vision, and slow-healing sores.

Heart disease is a condition that affects the heart and can be caused by a variety of factors, including high blood pressure, high cholesterol, smoking, and obesity. Symptoms may include chest pain, shortness of breath, and fatigue. Diagnosis typically involves a combination of physical examination, medical history, and diagnostic tests such as echocardiogram and electrocardiogram. Treatment options may include medications, lifestyle changes, and procedures such as coronary angiography and cardiac catheterization.
